title: Add Employee modal does not show validation or error feedback for invalid input
preconditions: User is logged in and on the dashboard.
steps_to_reproduce: Open Add Employee, enter invalid or incomplete data, and click Save.
actual_result: Nothing clear is shown in the UI. In some cases the modal just sits there, and in others the save fails without telling the user what went wrong.
expected_result: The form should block the save and show a clear validation or error message in the modal.
evidence: Cucumber failures expect a visible error message but get none. This is also consistent with the manual findings.
notes: Merged from the missing-validation and missing-error-message findings because they point to the same user problem.

title: Dependants field accepts invalid values and may save the wrong value
preconditions: User is logged in and on the dashboard.
steps_to_reproduce: Open Add Employee, enter invalid dependants values like decimals, mixed text and numbers, or other invalid formats, then save.
actual_result: The field is handled inconsistently. Some invalid entries are accepted, and the saved record may end up with a different dependants value than what was typed.
expected_result: Dependants should only accept valid whole numbers in range and should reject everything else before saving.
evidence: UI-F-012 shows invalid cases being persisted. The report includes examples where decimal input ended up saved as 1 and mixed input ended up saved as 12.
notes: This is specifically an Add flow issue. The Edit flow for invalid dependants is currently passing.

title: Name fields accept numeric or mixed values and still save
preconditions: User is on the dashboard or has valid API auth.
steps_to_reproduce: Try creating an employee with values like 12345, Ana123, or Lopez123 in the name fields.
actual_result: The invalid values are accepted and persisted.
expected_result: The system should reject invalid name formats and return clear validation feedback.
evidence: Automation captured successful saves for invalid names in both UI and API coverage.
notes: This was confirmed in both layers, so it is stronger than a UI-only finding.

title: First Name and Last Name columns are reversed in the dashboard
preconditions: At least one employee exists with clearly different first and last names.
steps_to_reproduce: Load the dashboard and compare the displayed values under First Name and Last Name.
actual_result: The Last Name column shows the first name, and the First Name column shows the last name.
expected_result: Each column should show the correct employee field.
evidence: UI-F-011 failed with expected last name Zulu but received Alpha instead. Embedded evidence in the report shows the same swap.

title: Dashboard table is not responsive on smaller viewports
preconditions: Dashboard has at least one employee row.
steps_to_reproduce: Resize the page to smaller viewport sizes and review the table layout.
actual_result: Part of the table becomes hard to use. Content can clip, shift, or look misaligned, especially with longer visible values.
expected_result: The table should stay readable and usable across practical viewport sizes.
evidence: The responsive and long-value review cases are marked FAIL and match the manual observation.
notes: Kept as one bug because the small-screen and long-value issues affect the same table layout.

title: Edit and Delete actions are not reachable with keyboard only
preconditions: At least one employee row exists.
steps_to_reproduce: Use keyboard navigation only and try to reach the Edit and Delete actions for a row.
actual_result: Keyboard focus does not reach those actions.
expected_result: Both actions should be reachable and usable with keyboard only.
evidence: Both accessibility checks failed in the report.

title: Add Employee modal inputs do not provide basic input hints
preconditions: User is logged in and can open Add Employee.
steps_to_reproduce: Open Add Employee and inspect the input fields.
actual_result: The inputs do not have non-empty placeholders or similar entry hints.
expected_result: The fields should provide basic guidance consistent with the product's form pattern.
evidence: ACC-NF-004 recorded null placeholders for first name, last name, and dependants.
notes: This came from the automation results rather than the original manual list.

title: Edit and Delete actions rely on icons that are not very clear
preconditions: At least one employee row exists.
steps_to_reproduce: Review the Edit and Delete row actions without hovering or using prior product knowledge.
actual_result: The controls rely on icons only, which makes them less clear than they should be.
expected_result: The actions should have clearer labels, tooltips, or stronger affordances.
evidence: This came from the manual review notes.
notes: This one should be rechecked with the current build before filing externally.

title: Default ordering in the dashboard is not clear to the user
preconditions: Multiple employees exist in the grid.
steps_to_reproduce: Create or review several records and inspect the order shown in the table.
actual_result: It is not obvious what the table is sorted by, and the order does not feel meaningful from a user point of view.
expected_result: The table should use a clear default sort and that sort should make sense for the user.
evidence: This came from the manual review notes.
notes: Better treated as a product or UX issue unless there is a documented sorting rule.

title: Technical ID shown in the main grid adds noise and hurts readability
preconditions: At least one employee row exists.
steps_to_reproduce: Review the main grid with normal and longer values.
actual_result: A long technical ID is shown in the main view even though it does not look useful for the user and adds clutter.
expected_result: Main table columns should focus on user-relevant data. Technical IDs should be hidden, shortened, or moved elsewhere.
evidence: The long-value layout review failed, and the manual feedback also called out the ID column as distracting.
notes: Low severity, but worth keeping as a UX cleanup item.

title: Save button styling looks destructive instead of confirmatory
steps_to_reproduce: Open the modal and review the visual treatment of the Save button.
actual_result: The Save action is styled in red, which reads more like delete or cancel than save.
expected_result: The Save action should use the standard primary or confirm styling.
evidence: This came from the manual review notes.

title: Expired session leaves the user stuck on the dashboard instead of returning to login
preconditions: User has an authenticated session that later expires.
steps_to_reproduce: Let the token expire, then refresh the page or go Home.
actual_result: The page stays on the dashboard in a broken state and the user is not sent back to login.
expected_result: Expired or invalid sessions should send the user back to login automatically.
evidence: This came from the manual review notes.
notes: Merged from the two session-expiry findings because they describe the same broken flow.

title: API accepts unexpected properties outside the contract
steps_to_reproduce: Send a create request with extra properties like middleName or other unknown fields.
actual_result: The API returns 200 and creates the employee instead of rejecting the request.
expected_result: The API should reject payloads that include properties not allowed by the contract.
evidence: API-F-011 shows status 200 for payloads that were expected to fail as client errors.

title: Deleted employee can still be retrieved by ID
preconditions: An employee exists and can be deleted.
steps_to_reproduce: Create an employee, delete it, then do a GET by the same ID.
actual_result: The follow-up GET still returns 200, even though the body is empty.
expected_result: After delete, GET by that ID should return a not-found style response, not success.
evidence: API-F-005 captured a 200 response after delete.

title: GET by invalid UUID returns 500 instead of a client error
steps_to_reproduce: Call GET by ID using invalid UUID formats like not-a-uuid or 12345.
actual_result: The endpoint returns 500 for bad client input.
expected_result: The endpoint should return a client error such as 400 for invalid IDs.
evidence: API-F-009 captured 500 responses for invalid IDs.

title: Invalid UUID error response is returned as HTML and exposes internal details
steps_to_reproduce: Call GET by ID using an invalid UUID and inspect the response body.
actual_result: The API returns an HTML error page with internal application details instead of a safe API error response.
expected_result: The API should return a sanitized JSON error without internal details.
evidence: API-F-009 includes an HTML error page starting with <!DOCTYPE html> in the response body.

title: API accepts employee creation without required username
steps_to_reproduce: Submit a create request with username missing or empty.
actual_result: The employee is created successfully even though username is supposed to be required.
expected_result: The API should reject the request with a validation error.
evidence: In API-F-006, missing firstName and missing lastName fail correctly, but missing username still returns 200.
notes: This confirms the manual concern that username handling is inconsistent.

title: API accepts username values longer than 50 characters
steps_to_reproduce: Submit a create request with username length 51.
actual_result: The API accepts the request instead of rejecting it.
expected_result: The API should reject username values above the maximum allowed length.
evidence: API-F-007 failed specifically on the username length 51 case.
